Functions for initializing, locking, enabling, and disabling peripheral configurations.

# Purpose
This C header file declares a set of functions for managing peripheral configurations in an embedded system. The function [`periph_config_init`](<#periph_config_init>) initializes the peripheral configuration system. The functions [`periph_config_acquire_lock`](<#periph_config_acquire_lock>) and [`periph_config_release_lock`](<#periph_config_release_lock>) manage access control by acquiring and releasing a lock, respectively. The functions [`periph_config_enable`](<#periph_config_enable>) and [`periph_config_disable`](<#periph_config_disable>) enable and disable a specified peripheral, identified by a pointer `periph` and a `rcc_bit`, which likely represents a bit in a register controlling the peripheral's clock. The file includes the standard library `<stdint.h>` to use fixed-width integer types.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stdint.h`
